title: Creating Tuples
description: Practice creating tuples in various ways. Build functions that construct tuples from individual values, wrap single values into one-element tuples (using the trailing comma syntax), and convert lists into tuples. These are foundational skills for working with immutable sequences in Python.
hints: A tuple is created by placing values separated by commas, optionally wrapped in parentheses: (x, y).
A single-element tuple requires a trailing comma: (value,). Without the comma, Python treats it as a parenthesized expression.
Use the built-in tuple() constructor to convert other iterables like lists into tuples.

testCases.description: create_single returns a single-element tuple, not the bare value
create_from_list converts a list to a tuple
create_single works with a string value
